title: Ensure that a Microsoft Entra diagnostic setting exists to  send Microsoft Entra activity logs to an appropriate destination

input_row.title: Ensure that a Microsoft Entra diagnostic setting exists to  send Microsoft Entra activity logs to an appropriate destination
input_row.description: Ensure that a Microsoft Entra diagnostic setting is configured to send Microsoft Entra activity logs to a suitable destination, such as a Log Analytics workspace, storage account, or event hub. This enables centralized monitoring and analysis of Microsoft Entra activity logs.
input_row.rationale: Microsoft Entra activity logs enables you to assess many aspects of your Microsoft Entra tenant. Configuring diagnostic settings in Microsoft Entra ensures these logs are collected and sent to an appropriate destination for monitoring, analysis, and retention.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Under Monitoring, click Diagnostic settings. 3. Next to each diagnostic setting, click Edit setting, and review the selected log categories and destination details. 4. Ensure that at least one diagnostic setting is configured to send the following logs to an appropriate destination: o AuditLogs o SignInLogs o NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs o ServicePrincipalSignInLogs o ManagedIdentitySignInLogs o ProvisioningLogs o ADFSSignInLogs o RiskyUsers o UserRiskEvents o NetworkAccessTrafficLogs o RiskyServicePrincipals o ServicePrincipalRiskEvents o EnrichedOffice365AuditLogs o MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs o RemoteNetworkHealthLogs o NetworkAccessAlerts
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Under Monitoring, click Diagnostic settings. 3. Click + Add diagnostic setting. 4. Provide a Diagnostic setting name. 5. Under Logs > Categories, check the box next to each of the following logs: o AuditLogs o SignInLogs o NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs o ServicePrincipalSignInLogs o ManagedIdentitySignInLogs o ProvisioningLogs o ADFSSignInLogs o RiskyUsers o UserRiskEvents o NetworkAccessTrafficLogs o RiskyServicePrincipals o ServicePrincipalRiskEvents o EnrichedOffice365AuditLogs o MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs o RemoteNetworkHealthLogs o NetworkAccessAlerts 6. Configure an appropriate destination for the logs. 7. Click Save.
input_row.impact: To export sign-in data, your organization needs an Azure AD P1 or P2 license. The amount of data logged and, thus, the cost incurred can vary significantly depending on the tenant size. See the following pricing calculations for respective services: • Log Analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost- logs#pricing-model. • Azure Storage: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/storage/blobs/. • Event Hubs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/event-hubs/.
input_row.default_value: By default, Microsoft Entra diagnostic settings do not exist.
input_row.justification: The control is specific to Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) diagnostic settings, which are not applicable to AWS services. The first AI opinion incorrectly suggested using AWS services like CloudWatch Logs, which do not apply to Azure-specific configurations. The second AI correctly identified this mismatch, emphasizing the need for Azure-specific tools and APIs. AWS APIs, such as boto3 or AWS CLI, cannot be used to verify Azure configurations.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Access the Azure Portal and navigate to Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Under the Monitoring section, click on Diagnostic settings. 3. Review each diagnostic setting by clicking Edit setting and ensure that the following logs are configured to be sent to an appropriate destination: AuditLogs, SignInLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, ServicePrincipalSignInLogs, ManagedIdentitySignInLogs, ProvisioningLogs, ADFSSignInLogs, RiskyUser. 4. Verify that the logs are being sent to a suitable destination such as a Log Analytics workspace, storage account, or event hub for centralized monitoring and analysis.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control can be queried using Azure APIs as it relates to the configuration of diagnostic settings in Microsoft Entra (Azure AD). 
- The audit field provides sufficient guidelines for checking the diagnostic settings, and the references include specific documentation on how to configure these settings. 
- Azure SDK for Python and Azure CLI can be utilized to automate the verification of the existence and configuration of the diagnostic settings.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_entra_id_diagnostic_setting_activity_logs_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

1. Use Azure SDK methods to access Microsoft Entra diagnostic settings:
   - Utilize `azure.identity` library for authentication and `azure.mgmt.monitor` for accessing diagnostic settings.
   - Specifically, use `MonitorManagementClient` class to retrieve diagnostic settings for Microsoft Entra.

2. Azure CLI commands:
   - `az monitor diagnostic-settings list --resource <resource_id>` to list the diagnostic settings for the specific Microsoft Entra resource.
   
3. Identify resources to enumerate:
   - The program will check the diagnostic settings applied to the Microsoft Entra ID resource within particular subscriptions and resource groups.

4. Configuration properties/settings to validate:
   - Ensure that the required log categories are enabled: 
     - AuditLogs
     - SignInLogs
     - NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
     - ServicePrincipalSignInLogs
     - ManagedIdentitySignInLogs
     - ProvisioningLogs
     - ADFSSignInLogs
     - RiskyUsers
     - UserRiskEvents
     - NetworkAccessTrafficLogs
     - RiskyServicePrincipals
     - ServicePrincipalRiskEvents
     - EnrichedOffice365AuditLogs
     - MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs
     - RemoteNetworkHealthLogs
     - NetworkAccessAlerts
   
5. Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass: At least one diagnostic setting with the above log categories configured to an appropriate destination.
   - Fail: No diagnostic settings found or log categories not configured properly.

By employing the Azure APIs and CLI tools, the automated control can efficiently assess whether the required diagnostic settings for Microsoft Entra activity logs are in place, ensuring compliance with organizational security posture and logging requirements.
